featureName: BUG-038-001: Google Drive provider used http.DefaultClient (no timeout); a hung endpoint blocks the scan/monitor goroutine indefinitely

executionHistory.summary: Sweep round 17 (devops lens) of spec 038 found cmd/core/wiring.go injects http.DefaultClient (no timeout) into the Google Drive provider, so a connect-but-never-respond endpoint blocks the scan/monitor goroutine indefinitely. Every other connector sets an explicit client timeout. Verified by reading the wiring + all p.client.Do call sites.

Single bugfix-fastlane scope: add a fail-loud SST response-header-timeout key and build a bounded Transport (NOT a whole-request timeout, which would abort large downloads). SCN-038-TIMEOUT-01/02 authored.

Added drive.providers.google.http_response_header_timeout_seconds to config/smackerel.yaml + scripts/commands/config.sh (assignment + heredoc emit); parsed fail-loud in internal/config/drive.go into HTTPResponseHeaderTimeoutSeconds; built the Google provider client in cmd/core/wiring.go with Transport.ResponseHeaderTimeout + dial + TLS-handshake timeouts from the SST value, no whole-request Timeout.

Wired the new required key into driveSSTKeys + setRequiredEnv + a populate assertion. TestDriveConfigValidationRequiresEverySSTField/...HTTP_RESPONSE_HEADER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS proves fail-loud; TestDriveConfigPopulatesEveryField asserts == 30. go build ./... + vet green.
Full internal/config package green (ok 31.375s) with the new required key. ./smackerel.sh config generate resolves the key into dev.env (config-validate OK).
One SST key + one bounded Transport; reuses already-imported net/http/time; no new abstractions.
ResponseHeaderTimeout bounds the hang without capping large downloads; pool tuning matches the discord precedent. No new perf hot path.
Bounding an unbounded client reduces a hang/DoS surface. No secret handling change; the bearer token is still never logged. No framework files touched.
artifact-lint PASSED for the packet; config generate + build + vet + full config package green. Evidence in report.md.
Diff confined to the SST source + config loader/tests + wiring client construction; no migration; generated env gitignored; no .github/bubbles changes; parent spec 038 artifacts untouched.
smackerel.yaml + drive.go + wiring.go comments document the response-header-timeout contract and the why-not-whole-request rationale. Unused rate-limit enforcement flagged for the owner.

scopeProgress.title: Bound the Google Drive provider client (fail-loud SST timeout)

lockdownState.reason: bugfix-fastlane config+wiring fix; no convergence loop required

resolvedFindings.summary: cmd/core/wiring.go injected http.DefaultClient (no timeout) into the Google Drive provider, so a hung endpoint blocked the scan/monitor goroutine indefinitely.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Added fail-loud SST key drive.providers.google.http_response_header_timeout_seconds; built the provider client with Transport.ResponseHeaderTimeout + dial + TLS timeouts from it (no whole-request Timeout, so large downloads are not aborted). Config tests lock the required key; config generate resolves it end-to-end.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md Test/Validation Evidence; internal/config/drive.go parse; cmd/core/wiring.go Transport; dev.env DRIVE_PROVIDER_GOOGLE_HTTP_RESPONSE_HEADER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=30.

discoveredIssues.summary: drive.rate_limits.requests_per_minute is SST-declared and parsed but not enforced by a runtime limiter. Enforcing it is a feature (token-bucket), out of scope for this reliability fix.
